one_line_definition: Ultrasonic velocity measurement technique driving oscilloscope x-axis at reciprocal of travel time so successive echoes overlap
Phase shift of ultrasonic beam due to wave diffraction near transducer that must be subtracted for accurate velocity measurement
Time taken for an ultrasonic pulse to travel between two points; basis for distance and velocity measurement
TOF method that computes conjugate product of two echo spectra and takes IFFT; peak of result gives delay; robust for attenuating media
TOF method computing group delay from slope of unwrapped cross-spectral phase vs. frequency (linear phase assumption)
Splitting of a shear wave into fast and slow polarisations in a stressed anisotropic material; magnitude proportional to stress
Frequency-domain analysis of broadband ultrasonic echoes; spectral minima spacing reveals differential shear-wave arrival time
Dependence of ultrasonic velocity on mechanical stress; basis for stress measurement via wave speed change
Influence of the liquid couplant thickness between transducer and specimen on waveform shape and TOF accuracy
TOF reference taken from first zero-crossing of received waveform; more reproducible than peak-picking for some setups
Region close to a transducer face (z < a²/λ) where pressure field is complex with multiple maxima and minima
Region distant from the transducer (z >> a²/λ) where beam becomes regularly diverging and far-field approximations apply
Characteristic distance separating near and far field, N = D²f/(4c) = D²/(4λ); actual transition for beam regularity is ~0.75 N
Idealised model of a flat circular radiating element oscillating with uniform velocity; foundation for near-field beam calculations
Method decomposing a transducer's radiated field into a superposition of plane waves, propagated individually and reassembled
Angular spectrum implementation using 2D FFT; fast but susceptible to aliasing near the evanescent boundary
Angular spectrum implementation via direct spatial convolution; more accurate in central region but slower
Scattering framework relating incident plane-wave expansion to scattered field; used in near-field measurement model
High-frequency asymptotic evaluation of oscillatory integrals by expanding phase around stationary points
Standard far-field pulse-echo ultrasonic measurement model relating received voltage to far-field scattering amplitude
NDT method for concrete plate thickness using thickness resonance frequency of impact-generated waves (S1-ZGV mode)

aliases_seen: zero group velocity mode; thickness resonance
one_line_definition: First symmetric Lamb mode at its zero-group-velocity frequency; peak in frequency spectrum used for IE thickness estimation
Seismic/NDT method extracting shear-wave velocity profile from multichannel surface-wave recordings
Guided elastic wave in a plate; symmetric (S) and antisymmetric (A) modes; foundation for IE and MASW thickness methods

aliases_seen: diffraction time delay; diffraction phase delay
one_line_definition: Phase/time delay imposed on a received ultrasonic signal by beam diffraction; varies with path length, frequency, and transducer size
Instrument measuring gas/liquid flow velocity from differential transit times of upstream/downstream ultrasonic pulses
Measured absolute sound speed in a fluid; used in USMs for mass/energy flow and density determination
Pre-installation calibration of USM transducer time delays without flowing medium present
Non-contact sensor based on C = ε₀εᵣA/d; measures displacement from capacitance change; sub-nm resolution with guard rings
Concentric electrode at same potential as sensing electrode, eliminating fringe fields for linear 1D capacitive sensing
Non-contact sensor exciting eddy currents in a conductive target; coil impedance change encodes distance; penetration depth δ = √(ρ/πfμ)
Depth to which eddy currents penetrate a conductor, δ = √(ρ/πfμ); decreases with increasing frequency
Interface IC converting capacitance to digital output; charge-balancing Σ-Δ type achieves 17-bit at 20 µs, 2.2 pJ/bit FOM
Change in eddy-current coil impedance due to curved target surface; negligible when R_surface/R_coil > 15 (convex) or 30 (concave)
Metrology technique separating machine (spindle) errors from artefact form errors using multiple measurements and mathematical combination
Requirement that the measurement scale be collinear with the measured displacement to avoid cosine errors
Low thermal expansion glass-ceramic material used for dimensional reference artefacts and mirrors
High-resolution length measurement based on optical interference; ~1 nm resolution in precision CMMs and comparators
Eleven technologies: search coil, flux-gate, optically pumped, nuclear precession, SQUID, Hall-effect, magnetoresistive, magnetodiode, magnetotransistor, fiber optic, magneto-optic
Most sensitive magnetic sensor (~10⁻¹⁴ T); operates at cryogenic temperatures; used for measuring very weak fields
Sensor using change in resistance of Permalloy thin film in a magnetic field; range 10⁻⁷–50 G; IC-compatible; DC to ~1 GHz
Sensor producing voltage proportional to magnetic flux through a semiconductor; 10⁻³–10³ G; DC to 1 MHz
Non-contact sensor using back-pressure or flow change of an air jet impinging on a target to indicate proximity
Pneumatic sensor nozzle modification adding a swirl chamber to extend jet attachment range and increase output pressure
Measurement of complex wavenumber in liquid foams via air-coupled ultrasound; reveals bubble size and dispersion
Low-frequency effective-medium limit for sound speed in a bubbly liquid: velocity collapses toward ~25 m/s for void fractions >0.5%
Transducer operating in air without liquid couplant; capacitance type used for foam characterisation at 60–600 kHz
Non-contact ranging using light; methods include interferometry, triangulation, TOF, confocal chromatic, white-light interferometry
Optical surface profiling using short-coherence light to gate depth by coherence peak position; ~nm resolution
Non-contact displacement using position of reflected laser spot on detector; µm–mm resolution over mm–m range
Acoustic focusing of a transducer beam; modes: FPF (flat plate focus), PTF (point target focus), OLF (optical limit); max focal length = 0.8N
Product of material density and sound speed; governs reflection/transmission at interfaces
